title: CS Lesson Plan (Teach First Proforma + Pedagogy Database alignment)
description: Schema for GCSE Computer Science lesson plans generated for the AI-Enhanced Personalised Education Service. Based on the Teach First Planning Proforma structure, extended with metadata linking each lesson to the project's DOK/AO/PRIMM/BKT skill model.

header.description: Direct equivalent of the proforma's top row (Date / Class / Unit of work).

date.description: Omit or leave null for template/bank lessons not yet scheduled.

class_group.description: Anonymised class identifier only — never an identifiable pupil list. Consistent with the privacy-first architecture.

duration_minutes.description: Should equal the sum of teaching_episode[].timing_minutes.

learning_sequence_context.description: Where this lesson sits in the scheme of work.

prerequisite_lesson_ids.description: lesson_id values that should logically precede this one.

next_lesson_link.description: What this lesson sets up for next.

pedagogical_metadata.description: Links this lesson to the Pedagogy Database / Assessment-Analysis-Composition Engine model. Split into deterministic fields (set at Stage 1 generation from the curriculum DAG) and moderated fields (set at Stage 2 moderation and reconciled per the 'moderation' object below). Structure varies by learning_strand — see allOf conditionals below.

learning_strand.description: Which curriculum strand this lesson belongs to. Determines which skill-ID field and which optional fields (DOK, AOs, notation) are meaningful for this lesson. ks3-ks4-programming-track spans Y9 Small Basic introduction through to GCSE Python and absorbs the existing five-stage GCSE algorithms progression.

notation_form.description: The representational abstraction used, independent of the specific tool. Deterministic — set from the curriculum seed.

tool.description: The specific software/language implementing notation_form, e.g. 'Flowol', 'Small-Basic', 'BASIC-256', 'Python', 'HTML', 'PowerPoint', 'Excel'. Deterministic. Kept as an open string with a controlled vocabulary maintained in the Pedagogy Database, rather than a closed enum, since new tools (e.g. a future BASIC-256 replacement) shouldn't require a schema change.

primm_phase.description: Deterministic — applies only within ks3-ks4-programming-track, fully determined by notation_form per the five-stage PRIMM mapping (pseudocode→Predict, flowchart→Run, text-code first contact→Investigate, text-code with scope constraints→Modify, full text-code→Make). Not a moderation target.

algorithm_skill_ids.description: ks3-ks4-programming-track only. Deterministic — BKT knowledge component IDs from the curriculum DAG, e.g. 'algo.selection.if-else'.

notation_skill_ids.description: ks3-ks4-programming-track only. Deterministic — BKT knowledge component IDs, e.g. 'notation.pseudocode.fluency', 'notation.small-basic.fluency'.

application_skill_ids.description: ks3-digital-literacy only. Skill IDs for GUI-application proficiency, e.g. 'app.mindmap.hierarchical-structure', 'app.powerpoint.slide-design', 'app.excel.formula-basic'. A distinct BKT dimension from algorithm_skill_ids — this is procedural software competence, not algorithmic reasoning.

concept_skill_ids.description: ks3-computational-foundations only. Skill IDs for Y8 foundational computing concepts, e.g. 'concept.binary.place-value', 'concept.flowol.sequence-control', 'notation.small-basic.fluency-intro'.

markup_skill_ids.description: ks3-web-markup only. Skill IDs for HTML structural competence, e.g. 'html.structure.semantic-tags', 'html.attributes.href'. Markup has no algorithmic-complexity dimension in the DOK sense — this strand does not use dok_level.

threshold_concept.description: Deterministic where the topic is a designated threshold concept in the curriculum DAG (e.g. Selection); otherwise a Stage 2 judgement call — set moderation.status accordingly.

rosenshine_principles.description: Which of Rosenshine's principles the segments visibly enact. Low-ambiguity — may be auto-detected at Stage 1 rather than routed through moderation.

dok_level.description: ks3-ks4-programming-track only (Webb's DOK is usable pre-GCSE, but only this strand's AO mapping ties it to exam-board weighting — see allOf below). Moderated — cognitive complexity demanded by the lesson's tasks, judged independently of the objective's verb wording.

range.description: [min, max] DOK span covered across the lesson's activities.

rationale.description: Why this level was assigned, tied to specific teaching_episode segments. Mandatory — an unrationalised DOK tag is not moderatable.

assessment_objectives.description: Moderated — board-specific AOs this lesson addresses, checked against the actual specification.

rationale.description: Why this AO applies, tied to specific segments.

misconception_ids.description: Moderated — references into the Pedagogy Database misconception catalogue this lesson is designed to surface or address.

moderation.description: Provenance and audit trail for the moderated fields above (dok_level, assessment_objectives, misconception_ids, and threshold_concept where not curriculum-determined).

status.description: unmoderated: Stage 2 not yet run. provisional: two independent raters agreed, not yet human-checked. disputed: raters disagreed, queued for human review. expert-assigned: human-confirmed.

raters.description: One entry per independent Stage 2 pass.

reconciled_by.description: Human identifier, or null if not yet reconciled.

key_vocabulary.description: Disciplinary literacy metadata (Tier 2/3 vocabulary), per EEF Improving Literacy in Secondary Schools.

polysemous.description: Flag words with a distinct everyday meaning, e.g. 'variable', 'argument'.

send_five_a_day.description: EEF SEND 'Five-a-Day' strategies deliberately built into this lesson.

teaching_episode.description: Ordered sequence of segments, mirroring the proforma's Do Now / Introduction / Model / Continued Learning / Review structure.

notation_used.description: Which notation/tool this segment operates in. Spans all four learning strands, not just the GCSE-track.

check_for_understanding.description: The proforma's 'Check' row, formalised as a hinge question per Wiliam (2015).

decision_rule.description: What the teacher does on strong vs weak response, e.g. 'advance if >80% correct, else reteach with worked example'.

evaluation.description: Completed after delivery. Null for lesson plans not yet taught.

reflection.description: Completed after delivery. Null for lesson plans not yet taught.
